content:
I use artificial intelligence almost every day, to explore images, test ideas, search for words, and sometimes push open doors in my imagination that I could not have opened alone.
I know its power.
I know the strange exhilaration of describing something that does not yet exist and watching it appear a few seconds later. I know how easily this technology can make us feel more capable, more inventive, almost limitless.
And perhaps that is precisely why I cannot look away from its darker side.
The conversation about AI is often framed as a contest between optimism and fear.
On one side, we are promised medical breakthroughs, personalized education, scientific discovery, greater productivity, and freedom from repetitive work.
On the other, we are shown the familiar nightmare of a machine becoming conscious and turning against its creator.
But I am beginning to think that both stories distract us from a quieter and more immediate danger.
